name: Deep Chocolate Torte with Coffee Buttercream
recipeIngredient: 9 oz Bittersweet chocolate, melted and cooled
3/4 cup Apricot preserves, heated to lukewarm
recipeInstructions:
1. Line two greased jelly-roll pans (15x10x1-inch each) with wax paper and butter and flour the paper, shaking off excess.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat egg yolks. Add 1-1/2 cups sugar slowly, beating, until mixture is thick and pale and forms a ribbon. Add chocolate and combine batter well.
3. In another large bowl with clean beaters, beat egg whites until they hold soft peaks. Add remaining 1/4 cup sugar slowly, beating until whites hold stiff peaks. Stir in 1/4 of the whites into yolk mixture, gently fold in remaining whites, and pour batter into pans, spreading evenly.
4. Bake cake layers in middle of preheated 375°F oven for 18-20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Let cool in pans, covered with kitchen towels on large racks.
5. Invert cake layers onto racks and remove wax paper carefully.
6. Buttercream: Dissolve sugar in 1/3 cup water over moderate heat in small heavy saucepan, stirring. Bring to boil and continue boiling until registers 240°F on candy thermometer.
7. In a large bowl with electric mixer, beat eggs until frothy. Add sugar syrup slowly, beating, until mixture is cool. Beat in butter one tablespoon at a time. Beat in espresso powder and pinch of salt. Beat until well combined.
8. If necessary, chill buttercream, covered, until firm enough to spread.
9. Ganache: In large heavy saucepan, bring cream just to boil and remove from heat. Add chocolate and stir until smooth. Transfer mixture to a bowl and chill, covered, stirring occasionally, until thickened but still soft.
10. Assembly: Sift preserves through fine sieve into bowl. Arrange cake layers on work surface. Trim edges and halve each layer lengthwise. On serving plate, arrange one layer, spread third of preserves over it, then third of ganache. Top with second layer and continue with preserves, ganache alternating until finished with a cake layer on top. Spread sides and top with thin buttercream, chill for 30 minutes.
11. Spread additional buttercream, leaving about 1/3 cup to make rosettes. With pastry comb or fork tines, score buttercream lengthwise. Draw knife crosswise across scored buttercream at intervals. Transfer remaining buttercream to small pastry bag fitted with decorative tip. Pipe rosettes on top and along long sides of torte, interspersing chocolate coffee beans. Chill.
12. Remove from refrigerator about 30 minutes before serving.
a 1989 Gourmet Magazine favorite
